message:
fix(fcp-protocol): hard-cap SymbolRequest::max_symbols at the verify boundary (resolves br-7p8rd)

Pre-fix, `SymbolRequest::new` accepted any caller-supplied `max_symbols`
(up to u32::MAX) and `verify()` only validated `missing_hint` size via
`validate_hint_bounds`. The deeper `validate_request` in fcp-mesh
DOES enforce the per-tier cap (DEFAULT_MAX_SYMBOLS_UNAUTHENTICATED =
32, DEFAULT_MAX_SYMBOLS_AUTHENTICATED = 1000), but that is defense in
depth, not the primary gate. A peer holding a valid signing key could
mint and sign `max_symbols = u32::MAX` requests; the receiver would
spend an Ed25519-verify cycle materializing the transcript before any
admission control gate fired.

Add `MAX_SYMBOLS_HARD_CAP: u32 = 2001` (sized to RaptorQ's per-object
decode-buffer headroom — see `max_symbols_with_headroom` in
fcp-raptorq's decoder; one source-symbol plus the repair-tail capacity)
as the absolute ceiling. NO request — authenticated or not, regardless
of zone policy — should be honored above this bound.

Two-pronged fix per the bead's "make construction/verification fail
closed" requirement:

  - `SymbolRequest::try_new(...)` returns `Result<Self, FrameError>`
    and refuses to build a request that the receiver's `verify()`
    would reject. Honest callers minting wire-bound requests should
    use this constructor instead of `new`.
  - `SymbolRequest::verify()` now rejects `max_symbols >
    MAX_SYMBOLS_HARD_CAP` BEFORE materializing the transcript or
    burning Ed25519-verify cycles, mirroring the existing
    `validate_hint_bounds` pre-check.

`SymbolRequest::new` stays infallible for backward compatibility with
the existing test/conformance fixtures that deliberately mint
out-of-policy values to exercise rejection paths.

Three regression tests:
  * symbol_request_verify_rejects_max_symbols_above_hard_cap
  * symbol_request_verify_accepts_max_symbols_at_hard_cap (boundary
    inclusive — cap is "above which", not "at or above")
  * symbol_request_try_new_rejects_above_hard_cap (paired with
    boundary-inclusive accept)

cargo test -p fcp-protocol: 22/22 SymbolRequest lib tests pass; full
crate suite (483 lib + 8 + 28 + 22 + 71 + 73 across binaries) green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
